title: tui::app::handle_slash_command — 660-line if-chain on raw strings
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Dimension: Structural Modularity / OCP
Location: src/tui/app.rs:586-1247 (approx 660 lines, 18 'if text ==' + 6 'text.starts_with' branches)
Violation: The TUI has its own slash-command dispatcher completely parallel to src/cli/repl/slash.rs (tracked in #232). Each branch re-hand-rolls parsing, argument splitting, and DisplayMessage construction. Adding a command requires editing a 1767-line God object. Cyclomatic complexity far exceeds the 15 threshold; cognitive complexity is unreadable. This is a DRY violation across the TUI/CLI seam — both call sites must be kept in sync manually.
Mandated Refactor: Extract a shared SlashCommandRegistry trait in src/commands/ consumed by both seam handlers. Define trait Command { fn name() -> &'static str; fn aliases() -> &'static [&'static str]; fn execute(ctx: &mut CommandContext, args: &str) -> CommandOutcome; }. Register each command (HelpCmd, ClearCmd, StatusCmd, RewindCmd, ...) as a unit-struct implementing the trait. The TUI dispatcher becomes a HashMap<&'static str, Box<dyn Command>> lookup. Resolves this duplication once, OCP-compliant for all future commands. Complements #232 which covers only the CLI side.
